assembly_instructions: 3D-printed myoelectric prosthetic forearm. A cylindrical custom-fit forearm socket holds the electronics — battery pack, control board, and skin-contact myoelectric sensor — under a magnetic-mount external cover. A rotating wrist unit connects the socket to the hand frame; five independently driven fingers (thumb + four others) extend forward. Each finger has its own small drive motor at its base (two representative motors modeled here). The entire shell is a matte-textured printed Nylon 12.

info.japanese_name: Open Bionics Hero Arm（筋電義手）
info.english_name: Open Bionics Hero Arm — Myoelectric Prosthetic Arm
info.summary: 3D-printed, direct-print, myoelectric below-elbow prosthesis from Open Bionics. EMG sensors on the residual limb translate muscle signals into six pre-programmed grips.
info.description: The Hero Arm is a 3D-printed myoelectric below-elbow prosthesis developed by Open Bionics (Bristol, UK). It replaces the hand and forearm of a transradial amputee with a custom-fit socket that is 3D-scanned from the residual limb, ensuring a personalized, comfortable fit. Two skin-contact EMG sensors on the inner wall of the socket pick up muscle signals from the remaining forearm; an embedded STM32-based control board classifies those signals in real time and drives five independent finger motors to execute one of several pre-programmed grip patterns (fist, tripod, pinch, hook, key-grip, point). A 180°-rotation wrist ring lets the user pronate/supinate the hand for natural posing. The powered parts sit under a magnetically-attached external cover that snaps off for battery swaps and comes in multiple colors and themed 'hero covers' (Iron Man, Star Wars, Frozen, etc.). The entire structural shell is printed in laser-sintered Nylon 12 for toughness, and Open Bionics publishes portions of the design to support clinical fitting and aftermarket customization.
info.operator: Fitted by partner clinics; used worldwide by transradial amputees including pediatric users
info.contract_value: ≈£10k (UK NHS reference pricing; varies by fit)
info.status: In production (covered by NHS in the UK, various insurers elsewhere)

facts.label: Type
facts.value: Below-elbow myoelectric prosthesis, five independently motored fingers

facts.label: Control
facts.value: Dual skin-contact EMG sensors → pre-programmed grip classifier

facts.label: Construction
facts.value: SLS laser-sintered Nylon-12 shell, off-the-shelf motors + battery + STM32 board

facts.label: Socket
facts.value: Fully custom — 3D-scanned from the residual limb

facts.label: Covers
facts.value: Magnetic swap-out external covers (many finishes, themed 'hero' skins)

facts.label: License
facts.value: Commercial product; design elements open for clinical integration

sources.title: Open Bionics — Hero Arm product page

parts.name: Forearm Socket
parts.role: Custom-fit cylindrical socket — 3D-scanned from the residual limb for a personalized, comfortable fit.

parts.name: Wrist Unit (180° rotation)
parts.role: Manually-rotated wrist joint — lets the user pronate/supinate the hand over 180°.

parts.name: Hand Frame (Palm)
parts.role: Printed Nylon-12 palm frame. Roots all five fingers and houses the per-finger drive motors.

parts.name: Thumb
parts.role: Opposed thumb — drives its own independent motor for pinch and tripod grips.

parts.name: Index Finger
parts.role: Index finger — often the 'point' or 'trigger' finger in Hero Arm grip programs.

parts.name: Finger Motor — Thumb
parts.role: Small DC gearmotor at the thumb root — independent actuation, one of five in the hand.

parts.name: Finger Motor — Index
parts.role: Small DC gearmotor at the index-finger root (representative — all four non-thumb fingers have their own).

parts.name: Control Board (STM32)
parts.role: STM32-based controller. Classifies EMG signals into grip commands and drives the finger motors. Shown above the socket for inspectability.

parts.name: Battery Pack
parts.role: Li-ion battery pack powering the motors and control board; swapped through the external cover.

parts.name: Myoelectric (EMG) Sensor
parts.role: Skin-contact surface EMG sensor on the socket interior — one of two that pick up forearm muscle signals.

parts.name: External Cover (magnetic)
parts.role: Magnetically-attached outer cover that snaps on over the forearm electronics. Available in many finishes and themed 'hero' skins.
